name: divideandconquer
description: Task decomposition and parallel execution engine. Analyzes any complex task, breaks it into atomic subtasks, builds a dependency graph (DAG), groups independent work into parallel execution waves, and dispatches agents concurrently for maximum throughput. Handles feature builds, bug investigations, refactors, research synthesis, migrations, and greenfield projects. Includes heuristics for challenging false dependencies, worktree isolation for file conflicts, and honest assessment of serial vs parallel tradeoffs.
slogan: Serial is the enemy. Parallel is the default.
author: RuneweaverStudios

tools.name: decompose
tools.description: Break a task description into atomic subtasks with dependency mapping

params.task: The full task description to decompose
params.dimensions: Comma-separated dimensions to analyze: code,research,tests,config,docs (default: all)

tools.name: plan_waves
tools.description: Given a dependency graph, compute optimal parallel execution waves using topological sort

params.max_concurrency: Maximum agents to run in parallel per wave (default: unlimited)

tools.name: execute
tools.description: Execute the wave plan by dispatching agents for each wave. In Claude Code, uses Agent tool. In API/OpenClaw, uses sessions_spawn.

params.wave_plan: JSON wave plan from plan_waves output
params.agent_routing: JSON map of subtask IDs to agent types (optional)
params.dry_run: If true, output the plan without executing (default: false)

tools.name: analyze
tools.description: Analyze a task and output the full decomposition, dependency graph, wave plan, and speedup estimate without executing

params.output_format: Output format: text, json, or markdown (default: markdown)
